title: Wife of Maine father who died saving his children opens up about their ‘love story’
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Ryan Jennings, 46, from North Yarmouth, Maine, drowned saving his children from a Florida riptide on April 1.
Emily Jennings shared their love story and Ryan's heroism in a Facebook tribute, highlighting his role as a devoted father.
Two GoFundMe pages have collectively raised over $242,000 to support the Jennings family after Ryan's death.

title: A Vermont ski lift is being retired after six decades
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: The Outpost Double chairlift at Pico Mountain, Vermont, retired after nearly 60 years due to mechanical failure.
An engineering review deemed the lift inoperable; unique parts are difficult to obtain for repairs.
Pico Mountain plans to hold a summer auction for the Outpost chairs, seeking temporary replacement solutions.

title: Did you follow the local news this week? Take our Greater Boston news quiz.
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Boston.com offers a news quiz to test knowledge of recent Greater Boston stories.
Peter Chianca has been Boston.com's general assignment editor since 2019.
The quiz covers trending topics and local news events in Greater Boston.

title: Jayson Tatum returns to MSG, but Knicks hold off Celtics late: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to MSG after an Achilles injury, but the Celtics lost 112-106 to the Knicks.
Josh Hart led the Knicks with 26 points, shooting 5-for-7 from three-point range.
Payton Pritchard contributed 23 points for the Celtics in a game without Jaylen Brown.

title: His brother passed away from glioblastoma. He’s running Boston for cancer research
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Colin Gerner, 34, from Hoboken, NJ, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon in memory of his brother who died from glioblastoma.
Gerner co-founded StacheStrong, raising millions for brain cancer research, and is the No. 2 global fundraiser for the marathon.
StacheStrong announced a $1 million, five-year grant with the V Foundation to support glioblastoma research at Massachusetts General Hospital.

title: Rapper Flavor Flav donates sports equipment to Cambridge youth center
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Rapper Flavor Flav donated sports equipment to Frisoli Youth Center in Cambridge, supporting after-school and evening programs.
The donation included basketballs, volleyballs, soccer balls, and other sports gear, in partnership with Harvard College and Franklin Sports.
Frisoli Youth Center offers activities for children in 4th to 8th grade and teens aged 14 to 19 in Cambridge.

title: Jaylen Brown has a candid answer when asked if he deserves NBA MVP this season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown is averaging 28.8 points, 7.0 rebounds, and 5.2 assists per game for the Boston Celtics.
Brown helped the Celtics secure a 54-26 record and the No. 2 seed in the Eastern Conference.
Despite not being an MVP favorite, Brown emphasizes his leadership and performance contribution this season.

title: Special World Cup ticket sales shatter Commuter Rail records in first day
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Over 17,450 round-trip World Cup train tickets sold in a day; nearly 11,000 in the first hour.
MBTA plans to transport up to 20,000 passengers to seven World Cup matches at Gillette Stadium.
Boston Stadium special tickets permit unlimited Commuter Rail travel on match days for $80 each.

title: The Boston Fleet are ready to take a sold-out TD Garden by storm
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Fleet to play first PWHL game at sold-out TD Garden against Montreal Victoire on Saturday.
Olympic gold medalists Megan Keller, Aerin Frankel, and Haley Winn to feature prominently for the Fleet.
Fleet and Victoire are top two teams in PWHL standings, both on five-game win streaks.

title: Fall River woman who led sex trafficking ring with 5 others pleads guilty
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Christy Parker, 28, of Fall River pleaded guilty to leading a sex trafficking ring exploiting minors and an adult.
Parker used coercion, including violence and intimidation, to force victims into sex work in multiple states during 2023.
Five of the six indicted defendants pleaded guilty; Parker faces up to life imprisonment for her charges.
